{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "IT Officer, Nigeria",
  "description": "PATH is a global organization that works to accelerate health equity by bringing together public institutions, businesses, social enterprises, and investors to solve the world&rsquo;s most pressing health challenges. With expertise in science, health, economics, technology, advocacy, and dozens of other specialties, PATH develops and scales solutions including vaccines, drugs, devices, diagnostics, and innovative approaches to strengthening health systems worldwide.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position:&nbsp;IT Officer, Nigeria

Job Requisition ID:&nbsp;JR2532
Job Location:&nbsp;Abuja (FCT)

Description


	PATH seeks to recruit an IT Officer who will be responsible for providing support to end-users, including staff based in Abuja and field sites, in computer networks, maintenance of the computers, servers, UPS, printing equipment, and networking infrastructure.&nbsp;
	The IT Officer will be based in Abuja and will work alongside the larger International IT team and the PATH Nigeria team.
	The role will report to the Lead Regional System Administrator and will require periodic travel to field programs under the supervision of the Lead RSA.


Specific responsibilities


	Handle staff IT support requests and assist HQ IT engineers with network and server troubleshooting.
	Conduct periodic one-on-one and small group training sessions to ensure the successful adoption and ongoing effective use of PATH&rsquo;s IT solutions, including Office 365, MS Teams, One-Drive, BOX, and SharePoint.
	Conduct introductory sessions for new staff to familiarize them with IT solutions and policies.
	Ensure data backups are running reliably and that data is secure.
	Maintain staff PCs and printers.
	Keep the supervisor fully informed daily of all IT problems and advise on what steps are being taken to resolve any problems.
	Convene quarterly meetings to brief the supervisor, discuss any performance issues and provide information and feedback concerning IT operations in the project.
	Use the global helpdesk ticketing system to log all support requests and document them fully.
	Schedule preventive maintenance of all IT equipment, and supervise the same.
	Maintain an up-to-date inventory of all IT equipment, both on- and off-site.
	Liaise with the ISP to ensure continued availability of the Internet.
	Any other IT-related assignment as determined by the Lead, Regional Systems Administrator or Site Admin.


Education


	Bachelor&rsquo;s Degree in IT/Computer Science or any related field.&nbsp;
	At least 3 years of relevant experience in a similar position.&nbsp;
	Proven experience in mobile technology.&nbsp;
	Microsoft and/or Cisco certification are desirable.&nbsp;
	Demonstrate progressive experience and responsibilities as an IT professional.


Required Skills:


	Excellent PC troubleshooting skills.
	Proven expertise in IT network administration.&nbsp;
	Ability to accurately and completely log incidents in a ticketing system.&nbsp;
	Experience in conducting personal and small group IT training sessions and a passion for teaching people about new technologies.&nbsp;
	Basic server and network troubleshooting skills; the ability to follow instructions when acting as remote hands for HQ engineering staff.&nbsp;
	Understanding wired and wireless LANs, VPNs, and the ability to diagnose network issues using commonly available tools such as traceroute, ping, and pathping.&nbsp;
	Knowledge in Virtualization and Cloud infrastructure management.&nbsp;
	Good communication skills. Ability to travel up to 15% of allocated time. Candidates must have legal authorization to work in Nigeria.
",
  "datePosted": "2026-04-15",
  "validThrough": "2026-05-16",
  "employmentType": "Full-Time",

IT Officer, Nigeria Job at PATH Nigeria




        
    

        
        PATH is a global organization that works to accelerate health equity by bringing together public institutions, businesses, social enterprises, and investors to solve the world’s most pressing health challenges. With expertise in science, health, economics, technology, advocacy, and dozens of other specialties, PATH develops and scales solutions including vaccines, drugs, devices, diagnostics, and innovative approaches to strengthening health systems worldwide.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: IT Officer, Nigeria

Job Requisition ID: JR2532
Job Location: Abuja (FCT)

Description
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	PATH seeks to recruit an IT Officer who will be responsible for providing support to end-users, including staff based in Abuja and field sites, in computer networks, maintenance of the computers, servers, UPS, printing equipment, and networking infrastructure. 
	The IT Officer will be based in Abuja and will work alongside the larger International IT team and the PATH Nigeria team.
	The role will report to the Lead Regional System Administrator and will require periodic travel to field programs under the supervision of the Lead RSA.


Specific responsibilities


	Handle staff IT support requests and assist HQ IT engineers with network and server troubleshooting.
	Conduct periodic one-on-one and small group training sessions to ensure the successful adoption and ongoing effective use of PATH’s IT solutions, including Office 365, MS Teams, One-Drive, BOX, and SharePoint.
	Conduct introductory sessions for new staff to familiarize them with IT solutions and policies.
	Ensure data backups are running reliably and that data is secure.
	Maintain staff PCs and printers.
	Keep the supervisor fully informed daily of all IT problems and advise on what steps are being taken to resolve any problems.
	Convene quarterly meetings to brief the supervisor, discuss any performance issues and provide information and feedback concerning IT operations in the project.
	Use the global helpdesk ticketing system to log all support requests and document them fully.
	Schedule preventive maintenance of all IT equipment, and supervise the same.
	Maintain an up-to-date inventory of all IT equipment, both on- and off-site.
	Liaise with the ISP to ensure continued availability of the Internet.
	Any other IT-related assignment as determined by the Lead, Regional Systems Administrator or Site Admin.


Education


	Bachelor’s Degree in IT/Computer Science or any related field. 
	At least 3 years of relevant experience in a similar position. 
	Proven experience in mobile technology. 
	Microsoft and/or Cisco certification are desirable. 
	Demonstrate progressive experience and responsibilities as an IT professional.


Required Skills:


	Excellent PC troubleshooting skills.
	Proven expertise in IT network administration. 
	Ability to accurately and completely log incidents in a ticketing system. 
	Experience in conducting personal and small group IT training sessions and a passion for teaching people about new technologies. 
	Basic server and network troubleshooting skills; the ability to follow instructions when acting as remote hands for HQ engineering staff. 
	Understanding wired and wireless LANs, VPNs, and the ability to diagnose network issues using commonly available tools such as traceroute, ping, and pathping. 
	Knowledge in Virtualization and Cloud infrastructure management. 
	Good communication skills. Ability to travel up to 15% of allocated time. Candidates must have legal authorization to work in Nigeria.
    

    

        Click here to Apply  
        

    
     




        
        

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});

  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Business Development Manager",
  "description": "Emador Nigeria Integrated Resources is&nbsp;recruiting suitable and experienced candidates to fill the position below:

Job Position: Business Development Manager

Job Location:&nbsp;Ikeja, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary


	We are looking for an ambitious and energetic Business Development Manager to help us expand our clientele.


Responsibilities


	Develop a growth strategy focused on both financial gain and customer satisfaction
	Conduct research to identify new markets and customer needs
	Arrange business meetings with prospective clients
	Promote the company&rsquo;s products/services, addressing or predicting clients&rsquo; objectives
	Prepare Sales Contracts, ensuring adherence to law-established rules and guidelines
	Keep records of sales, revenue, invoices, etc.
	Provide trustworthy feedback and after-sales support
	Build long-term relationships with new and existing customers
	Develop entry-level staff into valuable salespeople.


Requirements


	B.Sc in business administration, sales, or a relevant field.
	Proven working experience as a business development manager, sales executive, or a relevant role
	Proven sales track record
	Experience in customer support is a plus
	Proficiency in MS Office and CRM software (e.g., Salesforce)
	Proficiency in English
	Market knowledge
	Communication and negotiation skills
	Ability to build rapport
	Time management and planning skills.


Salary
N150,000 - N200,000 per month.

Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to:&nbsp;integratedresourcesemadorniger@gmail.com&nbsp;using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.",

Business Development Manager Job at Emador Nigeria Integrated Resources




        
    

        
        Emador Nigeria Integrated Resources is recruiting suitable and experienced candidates to fill the position below:

Job Position: Business Development Manager

Job Location: Ikeja, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary


	We are looking for an ambitious and energetic Business Development Manager to help us expand our clientele.


Responsibilities
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	Develop a growth strategy focused on both financial gain and customer satisfaction
	Conduct research to identify new markets and customer needs
	Arrange business meetings with prospective clients
	Promote the company’s products/services, addressing or predicting clients’ objectives
	Prepare Sales Contracts, ensuring adherence to law-established rules and guidelines
	Keep records of sales, revenue, invoices, etc.
	Provide trustworthy feedback and after-sales support
	Build long-term relationships with new and existing customers
	Develop entry-level staff into valuable salespeople.


Requirements


	B.Sc in business administration, sales, or a relevant field.
	Proven working experience as a business development manager, sales executive, or a relevant role
	Proven sales track record
	Experience in customer support is a plus
	Proficiency in MS Office and CRM software (e.g., Salesforce)
	Proficiency in English
	Market knowledge
	Communication and negotiation skills
	Ability to build rapport
	Time management and planning skills.


Salary
N150,000 - N200,000 per month.

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Front Desk Personnel",
  "description": "Centric Pharmacy Nigeria Limited is a trusted name in the healthcare and retail industry, dedicated to improving the well-being of our customers through quality pharmaceutical services and everyday essentials. Established with a vision to make healthcare accessible, affordable, and customer-centered, we combine the reliability of a modern pharmacy with the convenience of a fully stocked supermarket &mdash; all under one roof.

AtCentric Pharmacy Nigeria Limited&nbsp;we are more than just a pharmacy. We are a comprehensive health and lifestyle destination committed to serving individuals, families, and communities with care and excellence. Our operations are guided by the principles of integrity, professionalism, and customer satisfaction.&nbsp;We pride ourselves on offering a wide range of genuine pharmaceutical products, prescription medications, over-the-counter drugs, health supplements, personal care items, and groceries. Our team of licensed pharmacists and trained attendants ensure every customer receives personalized attention, professional guidance, and exceptional service.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position:&nbsp;Front Desk Personnel

Job Location:&nbsp;Ibadan, Oyo
Employment Type: Full-time

Responsibilities


	Receive and attend to clients/patients in a professional manner
	Manage appointments and inquiries
	Handle billing, documentation, and data entry
	Maintain a clean and organized front office environment.


Requirements


	Minimum of OND/HND/BSc in any relevant discipline
	Good communication and interpersonal skills
	Basic computer proficiency
	Prior experience in a healthcare or customer service role is an advantage.


Key Skills:


	Customer service and client relationship management
	Effective communication (verbal and written)
	Administrative and organizational skills
	Time management and multitasking
	Proficiency in Microsoft Office and basic data systems.


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to:&nbsp;contact@quickpickrx.com&nbsp;using the Job Position as the subject of the email.",

Front Desk Personnel Job at Centric Pharmacy Nigeria Limited




        
    

        
        Centric Pharmacy Nigeria Limited is a trusted name in the healthcare and retail industry, dedicated to improving the well-being of our customers through quality pharmaceutical services and everyday essentials. Established with a vision to make healthcare accessible, affordable, and customer-centered, we combine the reliability of a modern pharmacy with the convenience of a fully stocked supermarket — all under one roof.
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    



AtCentric Pharmacy Nigeria Limited we are more than just a pharmacy. We are a comprehensive health and lifestyle destination committed to serving individuals, families, and communities with care and excellence. Our operations are guided by the principles of integrity, professionalism, and customer satisfaction. We pride ourselves on offering a wide range of genuine pharmaceutical products, prescription medications, over-the-counter drugs, health supplements, personal care items, and groceries. Our team of licensed pharmacists and trained attendants ensure every customer receives personalized attention, professional guidance, and exceptional service.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Front Desk Personnel

Job Location: Ibadan, Oyo
Employment Type: Full-time

Responsibilities


	Receive and attend to clients/patients in a professional manner
	Manage appointments and inquiries
	Handle billing, documentation, and data entry
	Maintain a clean and organized front office environment.


Requirements


	Minimum of OND/HND/BSc in any relevant discipline
	Good communication and interpersonal skills
	Basic computer proficiency
	Prior experience in a healthcare or customer service role is an advantage.


Key Skills:


	Customer service and client relationship management
	Effective communication (verbal and written)
	Administrative and organizational skills
	Time management and multitasking
	Proficiency in Microsoft Office and basic data systems.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.    

    

        Click here to Apply

title: Job Vacancies at Centric Pharmacy Nigeria Limited
content:
Centric Pharmacy Nigeria Limited is a trusted name in the healthcare and retail industry, dedicated to improving the well-being of our customers through quality pharmaceutical services and everyday essentials. Established with a vision to make healthcare accessible, affordable, and customer-centered, we combine the reliability of a modern pharmacy with the convenience of a fully stocked supermarket — all under one roof.

At Centric Pharmacy Nigeria Limited we are more than just a pharmacy. We are a comprehensive health and lifestyle destination committed to serving individuals, families, and communities with care and excellence. Our operations are guided by the principles of integrity, professionalism, and customer satisfaction.
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    



We are recruiting to fill the following positions below:

1.) Laboratory Technician

Job Location: Ibadan, Oyo
Employment Type: Full-time

Responsibilities


	Perform routine laboratory tests and procedures
	Prepare samples and maintain accurate records
	Ensure proper use and maintenance of laboratory equipment
	Adhere strictly to standard operating procedures and safety guidelines


Requirements


	Minimum of a Diploma or Degree in Medical Laboratory Technology or related field with 2-5 years of experience
	Relevant professional certification is an advantage
	Prior experience in a diagnostic laboratory setting is preferred
	Strong attention to detail and organizational skills


Key Skills:


	Sample collection and handling
	Laboratory equipment operation and maintenance
	Data recording and documentation accuracy
	Knowledge of laboratory safety and quality control standards
	Analytical and problem-solving skills


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.

 

2.) Front Desk Personnel

Job Location: Ibadan, Oyo
Employment Type: Full-time

Responsibilities


	Receive and attend to clients/patients in a professional manner
	Manage appointments and inquiries
	Handle billing, documentation, and data entry
	Maintain a clean and organized front office environment.


Requirements


	Minimum of OND/HND/BSc in any relevant discipline
	Good communication and interpersonal skills
	Basic computer proficiency
	Prior experience in a healthcare or customer service role is an advantage.


Key Skills:


	Customer service and client relationship management
	Effective communication (verbal and written)
	Administrative and organizational skills
	Time management and multitasking
	Proficiency in Microsoft Office and basic data systems.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.

 

 

3.) Pharmacy Technician (Experienced)

Job Location: Ibadan, Oyo
Employment Type: Full-time

Responsibilities


	Assist the pharmacist in dispensing medications
	Manage inventory and stock control
	Provide basic medication guidance to customers
	Ensure compliance with regulatory and safety standards.


Requirements


	Certified Pharmacy Technician qualification
	Proven experience in a retail or hospital pharmacy setting
	Good knowledge of pharmaceutical products and inventory systems
	Strong customer service skills.


Key Skills:


	Medication dispensing and dosage accuracy
	Inventory and stock management
	Knowledge of pharmaceutical products and drug interactions
	Attention to detail and regulatory compliance
	Customer service and communication skills.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.    

    

        Click here to Apply
